name: Barrett Deems
abstract: Barrett Deems (March 1, 1914 – September 15, 1998) was an American swing drummer from Springfield, Illinois. He worked in bands led by Louis Armstrong, Jimmy Dorsey, Red Norvo, and Muggsy Spanier.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Woody Herman and His Third Herd/ Louis Armstrong and His All Stars
Kool Jazz Festival: Goin' to Chicago

about_quotes.speaker: Warren Vaché
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, I did my first overseas trip in 1974 with Benny and John Bunch and Bucky Pizzarelli, Barrett Deems
about_quotes.doc_title: Warren Vaché interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 23, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Zutty Singleton

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: But they also played good chord stuff, ‘cause that’s what Venuti insisted on. So, and oh Barrett Deems was the drummer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Quinn Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Barrett came in when Freddie Kohlman Coleman left... Barrett stayed with all the different bands that was there.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: Barrett Deems on drums, Art Hodes on piano and me on cornet. And we're do that for three months
